summary: The user demonstrated a highly structured and methodical approach to feature development, integrating new components, debugging issues, and ensuring code quality. They followed an iterative cycle of coding, verification, and problem-solving, consistently applying type-driven development and improving test coverage. The session concluded with proper version control and preparation for code review.
reconstructed_steps: Initiate Dashboard and API integration development.
Debug and verify initial dashboard functionality.
Develop and style the Product Card component.
Commit completed dashboard feature to version control.
Implement Shopping Cart feature with state management.
Resolve type definition issues and verify cart functionality.
Commit completed shopping cart feature to version control.
Improve test coverage for the cart context.
Finalize work by committing test improvements, pushing changes, and creating a pull request.

reasoning_steps.description: The user intended to start implementing the core dashboard functionality and anticipated the need for data fetching, thus opening both the UI component and the data source file.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 1: 'Opened `Dashboard.tsx` and `products.ts` to begin implementing core functionality.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user actively coded the planned feature, focusing on integrating data fetching logic into the dashboard and defining the API function.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 2: 'Implemented data fetching logic in `Dashboard.tsx` and created `getProducts` API function in `products.ts`.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user initiated the development server and refreshed the browser to visually verify the implementation of the new dashboard feature.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 3: 'Started development server (`npm run dev`) and refreshed browser to check UI.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user observed a runtime error related to data handling, indicating a problem with how fetched data was being processed or displayed.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 4: 'Encountered `TypeError` related to data mapping in `Dashboard.tsx`.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user recognized the nature of the error (async patterns in React's `useEffect`) and proactively sought external knowledge to diagnose and understand the underlying issue.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 5: 'Performed a web search for `useEffect` async patterns to diagnose the issue.'

reasoning_steps.description: Based on their diagnosis, the user implemented a solution to correctly handle asynchronous data fetching and manage loading states within the dashboard component.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 6: 'Applied a fix to `Dashboard.tsx` to handle async data fetching and loading states.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user confirmed that the applied fix resolved the issue and the dashboard was now functioning as expected.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 7: 'Verified the fix by refreshing the browser; dashboard loaded correctly.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user moved to develop a sub-component for displaying products, anticipating the need for both component logic and dedicated styling.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 8: 'Opened `ProductCard.tsx` and `ProductCard.module.css`.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user implemented the visual and interactive aspects of the product card, including layout and animations.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 9: 'Developed the `ProductCard` component and added responsive grid and hover animations via CSS.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user visually inspected the newly developed component in the browser to ensure its appearance met the design requirements.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 10: 'Visually verified the component's appearance in the browser.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user recognized the completion of a significant, logical unit of work (dashboard with product cards) and committed it to version control, following good SCM practices.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 11: 'Committed changes: 'feat: add product dashboard with cards'.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user initiated development of a new major feature (shopping cart), identifying the need for both UI presentation and centralized state management.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 12: 'Started work on the cart feature by opening `CartDrawer.tsx` and `CartContext.tsx`.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user actively coded the cart's user interface and its core business logic, including adding, removing, and calculating totals for items.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 13: 'Implemented the slide-out cart drawer UI and the `CartContext` with add/remove/total functions.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user encountered a type-related error, indicating that a required type definition was missing or incorrectly referenced.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 14: 'Encountered a `Type error: 'CartItem' not exported`.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user immediately addressed the type error by defining the necessary types in the central type definition file, demonstrating a commitment to type safety.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 15: 'Opened `types/index.ts` and added `CartItem` and `Product` type definitions.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user thoroughly tested the cart's functionality after the type fix, ensuring all operations (add, remove, total) worked correctly.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 16: 'Verified cart functionality (add, remove, total calculation) through multiple browser refreshes.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user completed another major feature (shopping cart) and committed it to version control as a distinct logical unit.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 17: 'Committed changes: 'feat: add shopping cart with context'.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user proactively checked the test coverage of their codebase, identifying an opportunity for improvement in code quality and reliability.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 18: 'Ran tests with coverage (`npm test -- --coverage`), achieving 78% coverage.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user decided to improve test coverage, specifically targeting the recently developed cart context, by adding more comprehensive test cases, including edge cases.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 19: 'Opened `CartContext.test.tsx` and added edge case tests for cart operations.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user verified that their efforts to add more tests successfully increased the overall test coverage.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 20: 'Re-ran tests, improving coverage to 89%.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user committed the test coverage improvements as a separate, logical change, maintaining a clean commit history.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 21: 'Committed changes: 'test: improve cart context test coverage'.'

reasoning_steps.description: The user prepared their completed work for integration by pushing the changes to the remote repository.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 22: 'Pushed changes to the remote repository (`origin/feature/shopping-cart`).'

reasoning_steps.description: The user initiated the code review process by creating a pull request on GitHub, signaling readiness for collaboration and potential deployment.
reasoning_steps.evidence: Action Sequence Step 23: 'Created a pull request on GitHub.'
